design_philosophy: E1: Emotion First, Functional Brutalism. This is a mission-critical War Room. No soft shadows, no glassmorphism. Hard edges, high contrast, dense data presentation, and immediate anomaly visibility. Color is used strictly for status and action.

spacing_and_layout.structure: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-4 h-screen p-4 box-border bg-[#050505]

panels.map: md:col-span-8 md:row-span-2 relative border border-border-default bg-black overflow-hidden h-[50vh] md:h-auto
panels.sidebar: md:col-span-4 md:row-span-2 flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-y-auto

surface_strategies.panels: GRID BORDERS - Flat background, 1px solid border, no rounded corners (rounded-none). The grid must be visible. Technical and rigid.
surface_strategies.alerts: THE TRACING BEAM - Critical alerts must have a glowing red (critical) or blue (copilot) 1px border. No soft shadows, use drop-shadow for neon effect.
surface_strategies.buttons: Neumorphism is BANNED. Use flat blocks of color with 1px borders. Hover state inverts colors or intensifies border.

map.markers: Use custom SVG icons for drones. Drone-1 turns red during EW attack. Draw a red dashed polygon for ROE (Rules of Engagement) boundaries.

telemetry_card.layout: Dense flex rows. Monospace fonts for all numeric data (Altitude, Battery, Signal).
telemetry_card.battery: Progress bar, 1px height. Turns red if < 20%.

ai_decision_card.appearance: High contrast. Border color: #007AFF. Uses typewriter effect for text to simulate real-time AI generation.
ai_decision_card.content: Actionable ROE-compliant route. Contains an 'APPROVE' button.
ai_decision_card.button: bg-[#007AFF] text-white font-bold uppercase tracking-widest py-3 px-6 w-full hover:bg-white hover:text-[#0A0A0C] transition-colors

ew_attack_button.appearance: bg-transparent border-2 border-[#FF3B30] text-[#FF3B30] font-black uppercase tracking-widest p-4 w-full hover:bg-[#FF3B30] hover:text-white transition-all
ew_attack_button.behavior: When clicked, triggers the GPS loss anomaly on Drone-1, updates state to critical, and activates the AI Copilot.

motion.general: Minimalist. Snap transitions. No bouncy springs.
motion.alerts: Flash background of Drone-1 panel red/black 3 times on EW attack.
motion.copilot: Fade in decision card, typewriter effect for text.

icons.usage: Use sharp, unrounded icons (Crosshair, ShieldAlert, Cpu, Radio, Navigation, Activity, Zap).

media.notes: No standard images. Rely on Map tiles and SVG data viz.

testing.attributes: ALL interactive and state-displaying elements MUST have `data-testid` attributes. Example: `data-testid='ew-attack-trigger'`, `data-testid='drone-1-status'`, `data-testid='approve-copilot-route'`.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use standard React with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn. The files are .js/.jsx, NOT .tsx.
Override all Shadcn border-radii to `rounded-none` to fit the tactical UI theme.
Install `lucide-react` for icons and `react-leaflet`, `leaflet` for the map.
Mock the map tiles using a standard dark tile provider: `https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png`.
Implement the 5-drone telemetry data as a mocked React state. Drone-1 is the primary target for the EW attack.
Create a 'Simulate EW Attack' button. On click, update Drone-1's GPS status to 'LOST' and signal to 'JAMMED'.

For the Ollama 7B AI recommendations, try to make a real fetch call to localhost:11434 if possible, but definitely implement a robust fallback/mock that simulates the AI response with a 2-3 second delay and typing effect if the local LLM is unreachable.
Ensure the layout strictly follows 'The Control Room' grid: dense, minimal scrolling, highly functional.
Add `data-testid` attributes to all actionable elements to comply with testing requirements.
